title: centunculus pentandrus var. elongatus
intro: Centunculus pentandrus var. elongatus, also known as Cornish heath or Cornish moneywort, is a flowering plant species. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cornish heath include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and potentially aiding in the management of conditions such as cardiovascular disease and diabetes. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically for its therapeutic benefits.
